title: Vost Tyne
content:

{{Quote|Now, suppose we discuss your Rebel friends, hm?|Vost Tyne|<ref name="PotM" />}}
'''Vost Tyne''' was a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] who ran a [[DSI Hyperbaride Synthesis Plant|hyperbaride synthesis plant]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Marca/Legends|Marca]]. Tyne served the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in its [[Navy/Legends|navy]] and, after attaining the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of admiral, began working very closely with the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. The two formed a close relationship, and Vader began training Tyne in the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. Tyne became proficient in many [[Force power/Legends|Force abilities]] under Vader's tutelage, including [[Telekinesis/Legends|Force Choke]], and soon tested his abilities against a [[Unidentified Jedi (Vost Tyne's victim)|novice Jedi]]. With Vader's assistance, Tyne was able to retire his commission in the Navy and gain a high position in a [[Hyperbaride/Legends|hyperbaride]] [[Hyperbaride synthesis plant|synthesis plant]] on Marca that had recently been taken over by the Empire. Once there, the [[Dark side adept/Legends|Dark Side Adept]] [[Assassin/Legends|assassinated]] the plant's [[Managing Director|managing director]], [[Arviz Linden]], and the head of its [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] security force, [[Gayle Sertim]], on suspicion of collaboration with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. After Tyne became the new managing director, he subjected the plant to his cruel methods and tightened the plant's security.

Tyne eventually came into conflict with a group of rebel agents who accidentally crashed on Marca. Though Tyne sent [[Swamp trooper/Legends|swamp troopers]] and [[repulsor scout]]s after them, the rebels continued to evade capture and infiltrated the plant with the intention of shutting it down. As the Alliance agents [[Assault on the DSI Hyperbaride Synthesis Plant|launched an attack]] with the help of the planet's native [[Sekct]], Tyne arrested Gayle Sertim's younger sisters, [[Marci Sertim|Marci]] and [[Darci Sertim|Darci]], who had betrayed him by assisting the rebels. While Tyne [[Prisoner/Legends|imprisoned]] Marci in one of the plant's detention cells, he took Darci to his personal suite and began interrogating her for information on the Alliance [[operative]]s. Tyne's interrogation was cut short as Marci escaped [[Prison/Legends|imprisonment]] and overloaded the plant's [[Hyperbattery|hyperbatteries]], initiating its self-destruction. Tyne fled through a secret [[Turbolift/Legends|turbolift]] to the plant's [[Hangar/Legends|landing bay]] and escaped Marca through [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] in a [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighter]], swearing vengeance against the rebels.

==Biography==
===Training and ascent===
{{Quote|As the enclosed progress report indicates, production of hyperbarides has increased dramatically since I assumed control of this plant from Arviz Linden.|Vost Tyne, in a report to Aaz'del Hutrick|<ref name="PotM" />}}
Vost Tyne was a [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]]. He eventually rose to the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Admiral/Legends|admiral]] and commanded a [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] in a [[Battle group/Legends|battle group]] assigned to work with [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]]. Tyne began working very closely with Vader, and the two were rumored to have developed a friendship. Tyne looked up to Vader as a mentor, and the Dark [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] soon began training him in the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]].<ref name="PotM" /> With the permission of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]],<ref name="Databank">{{DB|character|darthvader|Vader, Darth}}</ref> Tyne was trained to be fully [[Dark side adept/Legends|adept]] at dark side [[Force power/Legends|techniques]]. He soon put his abilities to the test and [[Death/Legends|killed]] an [[Unidentified Jedi (Vost Tyne's victim)|aspiring Jedi]], then took the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi's]] [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] as a trophy and taught himself how to [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|use]] it.<ref name="PotM" />

Tyne retired his commission from the Imperial [[Navy/Legends|Navy]] after the Empire took control of [[Dynamic Synergetics, Inc.]], a corporation that owned a [[DSI Hyperbaride Synthesis Plant|plant]] on the swamp [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Marca/Legends|Marca]]. The plant processed [[Hyperbaride/Legends|hyperbarides]], a group of rare super-heavy elements used in the production of [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolasers]], making it an asset to the Empire. With Vader's personal influence, Tyne was able to gain a position as the executive assistant to [[Arviz Linden]], the [[Hyperbaride synthesis plant|hyperbaride plant's]] [[Managing Director|managing director]]. Tyne's job was to keep an [[Eye/Legends|eye]] on Linden, who disliked many of the changes that the Empire brought to the plant.<ref name="PotM" />

Tyne discovered that, under Linden's [[Administrator/Legends|administration]], the plant had been working at only 25% of its total capacity, largely because Linden had sought to prevent undue ecological damage to Marca's environment and its native [[Species/Legends|species]], the [[Reptile/Legends|reptilian]] [[Sekct]]. The Empire, however, ordered Linden to bring production up to maximum capacity. Linden attempted to fight this directive but failed, and tried to contact the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] for aid. Discovering this, Tyne [[Telekinesis/Legends|Force Choked]] Linden to death and made his death look like an accident.<ref name="PotM" />

Tyne became the plant's new managing director and increased hyperbaride production dramatically, as he noted in a report to [[Director/Legends|Director]] [[Aaz'del Hutrick]] of the [[Imperial Department of Munitions Resource Development]]. Tyne included a complete survey of the [[Marcellus system]] in his report and argued that the increases in [[Imperial Military/Legends|military]] production and economic benefit for the Empire due to increased hyperbaride production more than justified any harm caused to Marca's ecosystem. The Managing Director demoted Linden's former personal assistant, the [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] [[M-3PO-C]], and installed an override in the plant's [[Computer/Legends|computer]] system with the intention of alerting the plant's security forces should the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] attempt to access databases below his clearance. M-3PO-C was replaced by [[D-3P-O2]], Tyne's loyal [[Secretary droid/Legends|secretary droid]], who worked outside of the Managing Director's office.<ref name="PotM" />

[[Gayle Sertim]], leader of the plant's [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] security force, believed foul play was involved in Linden's death. Tyne, aware of Sertim's previous loyalty to Linden and concern for the Sekct, kept her under close surveillance. After Tyne learned that Sertim had attempted to contact members of the Rebellion, he sent her off-planet on a [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttle]] equipped with a [[Power coupling/Legends|power coupling]] that was booby-trapped with a [[thermex charge]]. Tyne remotely detonated the charge and destroyed the [[Starship/Legends|ship]], killing Sertim. He fabricated a cover story blaming the incident on an attack by Rebel [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]], which was universally accepted, even by Sertim's younger sisters, [[Marci Sertim|Marci]] and [[Darci Sertim|Darci]], who took over their sister's position in leading the plant's civilian security force.<ref name="PotM" />

===Rebel incursion===
{{Quote|So we have some visitors to our facility. We must dispatch some troops to greet our visitors in the proper manner.|Vost Tyne|<ref name="PotM" />}}
[[File:TyneSertimSisters.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Tyne arrests Marci and Darci Sertim.]]
Sometime after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], a group of six Rebel agents accidentally crash-landed on Marca after attempting to take a shortcut through the [[Marcellus Nebula]] on their way to meet [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar]] in the [[Sumitra sector/Legends|Sumitra sector]]. Tyne was notified of the crash immediately by a [[Technician/Legends|technician]] and ordered a force of eight [[Swamp trooper/Legends|swamp troopers]] and two [[repulsor scout]]s to be sent out to investigate the crash site and capture any survivors. More [[Troop/Legends|troops]] and [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsor]] scouts were deployed once the Alliance agents persisted in evading capture. The plant was soon infiltrated by the Rebels, who decided to try and shut down the facility to save Marca and find a ship to escape the planet with. Once inside, the Rebels accessed the plant's computer systems with the help of a sympathetic M-3PO-C, who triggered Tyne's override and alerted the plant's security forces, which were able to capture the Rebels. The Alliance [[operative]]s were [[Prisoner/Legends|imprisoned]] and readied for interrogation.<ref name="PotM" />

Tyne soon discovered that the Rebels had escaped with the help of Marci and Darci Sertim, who had learned of their sister's true fate from the agents. The Rebels fled with weapons and repulsor scouts, mobilizing the Sekct for a [[Assault on the DSI Hyperbaride Synthesis Plant|final offensive]] against Tyne and the Imperials. Tyne had the sisters arrested in the plant's security control room and was able to eavesdrop on them and learn their plan using [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. He [[Prison/Legends|imprisoned]] Marci in the plant's detention cells, while he held Darci hostage in his personal suite. He decided to allow the Sekct to approach the plant, believing that the superior training of his troops would lead to a substantial number of Sekct casualties during the battle. Tyne drew his troops together and prepared them for close combat with the Sekct, arming them with whatever weapons had not been raided by the Alliance agents and the Sertim sisters. He left only a skeleton force inside the plant, confident that the Rebels would not be able to penetrate the facility, and summoned a detachment of six troops to serve as his personal guard.<ref name="PotM" />

While the battle between the Imperial forces and the alliance of Sekct, Rebels, and civilian mercenaries broke out outside, Tyne began interrogating Darci in his suite to gain information on the Rebel agents. After immobilizing Darci with [[Binders/Legends|binders]] around her wrists and ankles, Tyne used his Force abilities to inflict pain on the mercenary and directly [[Mind trick/Legends|affect her mind]]. Tyne halted the interrogation once the plant's warning system announced an overload of the plant's [[Hyperbattery|hyperbatteries]], which had been initiated by a recently liberated Marci. Tyne took his personal [[Turbolift/Legends|turbolift]] down to the plant's [[Hangar/Legends|landing bay]] in an attempt to escape the doomed facility while keeping Darci as a hostage. He eventually came into conflict with the Alliance agents and surrendered his weapon in a feint, using his Force powers against them and attempting to utilize Darci as a Human shield.<ref name="PotM" />

{{RPG}}
Tyne fled the self-destructing plant in a [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighter]] and jumped to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. He decided against reporting to the Imperial Military, not wanting to face the repercussions of the plant's destruction, and instead devoted his time to plotting his revenge against the Rebels.<ref name="PotM" />
{{Endgame}}

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Analysis of dead Sekct bodies indicates that the race is absorbing a great deal of the toxic emissions from the plant. They have a highly reactive genetic structure and my head researcher believes the species will die out within two generations. Naturally, this is a tolerable loss, at least to my way of thinking.|Vost Tyne, in a report to Aaz'del Hutrick|<ref name="PotM" />}}
[[File:TyneEscapes.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Tyne escapes aboard a TIE fighter.]]
Cold, calculating, and incredibly cruel, Vost Tyne managed to create an aura of intimidation and fear wherever he went, and those around him swore that his evil was a palpable presence. He was intelligent, ruthless, sly, and was good at turning any situation to his advantage through the use of trickery. He had a total disregard for life and was willing to commit genocide against the Sekct to increase hyperbaride production for the Empire. Tyne's voice was cold and hard, and he typically spoke in a brusque, military manner. He was a tall, thin, and austere individual with cold [[gray]] eyes, and as managing director, he wore a severe [[black]] outfit vaguely reminiscent of an [[Imperial military uniforms/Legends|Imperial military uniform]]. Tyne directly commanded the plant's Imperial security force.<ref name="PotM" />

Tyne's loyalty was always first to himself, and then to the Empire. He put his own safety above that of all others, and if D-3P-O2 reported a security incident at his office while the plant was on alert status, all personnel would be ordered to the area immediately to assist him no matter what else might have been going on. Security at the hyperbaride plant under Tyne's administration was very tight. A turbolift leading to the plant's landing bay, accessible only to Tyne through a magnetic key he kept on his person at all times, was placed in his luxurious suite. A powerful computer terminal was located in his office that allowed him access to all of the plant's systems, and the only control to the landing bay's roof door was also in his office.<ref name="PotM" />

Tyne was extremely cautious, keeping his executive quarters locked with a combination that only he knew. He kept two [[Hold-out blaster/Legends|hold-out blasters]] and a [[Heavy blaster pistol/Legends|heavy blaster pistol]] hidden in his quarters, as well as another hold-out [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] concealed beneath his pillow in his bedroom. Tyne had a very close, personal relationship with [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader, who helped him hone his Force skills and gain a high position at the plant. He kept a [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] of himself during his time as an admiral standing next to Vader in his personal quarters. Also located in his quarters, attached to a trophy base, was a fully functioning lightsaber that he had taken from a Jedi whom he had killed.<ref name="PotM" />

==Powers and abilities==
{{Quote|…We can kill Tyne—we'll surprise him so he can't use his abilities.|Darci Sertim|<ref name="PotM" />}}
Under the tutelage of his master, Darth Vader, Vost Tyne learned much about the dark side of the Force. He became proficient in many Force abilities, including those in the realms of [[Force power/Legends#Control|Control]], [[Force power/Legends#Sense|Sense]], and [[Force power/Legends#Alter|Alter]]. Those under his command at the plant believed him to be a strange sorcerer with powers evil beyond understanding. He used the Force to aid him in his interrogation of Darci Sertim, causing the mercenary pain and altering her mind. He was also able to use the Force to discreetly eavesdrop on the Sertim sisters and learn of their plan to destroy the plant. He used Vader's signature Force Choke on Arviz Linden, rupturing the Managing Director's [[trachea]] and killing him. Tyne taught himself how to use the lightsaber he took from a dead Jedi.<ref name="PotM" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|It was fun to take a more or less cookiee ''[sic]'' cutter villain and do something meaningful with him.|Abel G. Peña, on writing Tyne in the canceled ''Vader's Legacy'' article|<ref name="TFN" />}}
Vost Tyne was created for the ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' adventure ''[[Planet of the Mists]]'', written by [[Nigel D. Findley]] and published by [[West End Games]] in [[1992]], in which he appeared as the central villain. Tyne was illustrated by [[Tim Eldred]], who provided the interior artwork for the entire adventure. While all of the events described above occur in the adventure, many of the specific details and certain outcomes, such as Tyne's ultimate fate, are determined by the players. The character may die at the end of the adventure, or he may be kept alive to be used as a recurring villain in an ongoing campaign.<ref name="PotM" /> Tyne was later given a brief mention in Darth Vader's [[StarWars.com]] [[Databank (original)|Databank]] entry.<ref name="Databank" />

Tyne's relationship with Vader was to be expanded upon in the ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' article ''[[Vader's Legacy]]'' by [[Abel G. Peña]].<ref name="TFN">{{JCFCite|url=posts/25193305|board=Literature|thread="Vader's Legacy": by Abel & Joe (SWI #?)|user=[[Abel G. Peña|Halagad_Ventor]]|date=2006-10-01|time=2:56 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200801192655/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/vaders-legacy-by-abel-joe-swi.23237349/page-7}}</ref> However, [[Lucasfilm]] canceled the article, and it was never published.<ref name="NJOE">{{WebCite|url=http://www.njoe.com/2009/10/13/njoe-interviews-abel-g-pena|text=NJOE Interviews Abel G Peña…|date=2009-10-13|archivedate=20091119054755}}</ref>
